hct.source: Anemia (low hematocrit) is a common finding in rheumatoid arthritis due to chronic inflammation and iron deficiency.

hgb.source: Anemia (low hemoglobin) is a common finding in rheumatoid arthritis due to chronic inflammation and iron deficiency.

mch.source: Low mean corpuscular hemoglobin (MCH) suggests microcytic anemia, which can be seen in rheumatoid arthritis due to iron deficiency.

mchc.source: Low mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration (MCHC) suggests microcytic anemia, which can be seen in rheumatoid arthritis due to iron deficiency.

mcv.source: Low mean corpuscular volume (MCV) suggests microcytic anemia, which can be seen in rheumatoid arthritis due to iron deficiency.

plt.source: Elevated platelet count (PLT) can be seen in rheumatoid arthritis due to inflammation and thrombocytosis.

rbc.source: Low red blood cell count (RBC) suggests anemia, which can be seen in rheumatoid arthritis due to chronic inflammation and iron deficiency.

rdw.source: Elevated red cell distribution width (RDW) suggests increased variation in red blood cell size, which can be seen in early inflammatory processes like rheumatoid arthritis.

wbc.source: Elevated white blood cell count (WBC) can indicate inflammation and infection, which may be associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
